title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto announced the first copper cathode produced from its Nuton bioleaching technology at Gunnison Copper’s Johnson Camp mine in Arizona. The company says the process uses microorganisms to help extract copper from difficult sulphide ores and is designed to lower the carbon footprint of copper production.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper

title: Nornickel says nickel output flat year-on-year in first quarter
date: Monday, date not specified in search result
summary: Nornickel reported first-quarter nickel output of 42,000 metric tons, unchanged from a year earlier. The company also kept its full-year production forecast for nickel and other metals unchanged, indicating stable capacity guidance despite current output being flat.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, energy-saving method to extract nickel from low-grade ores using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon. The process is described as CO2-free, up to 18% more energy-efficient with renewable electricity and green hydrogen, and capable of producing refined ferronickel in a single reactor furnace.

title: Clean energy's nickel rush is heading straight for some of Earth's richest ecosystems
summary: This article highlights the environmental tradeoffs of meeting growing nickel demand for stainless steel and clean energy technologies. It cites new research warning that expanding nickel supply in tropical regions could threaten priority conservation areas.

title: Nickel batteries could further lose spark as Indonesian production costs surge
summary: The piece examines how rising production costs in Indonesia could weaken the competitiveness of nickel-based batteries. It suggests that nickel batteries may continue losing market share as key battery inputs become more expensive.

title: Tenaris acquires Artrom Steel Tubes for EUR86 million
summary: Tenaris announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Artrom Steel Tubes for €86 million. The article frames the deal as part of Tenaris's steel pipe business expansion, with Artrom described as a steel tubes producer.

title: Tenaris to acquire Romanian pipe maker Artrom for €86 million
summary: Tenaris said it entered into a definitive agreement to buy Artrom Steel Tubes S.A. for €86 million. The piece reports the transaction as a company news item centered on Tenaris's expansion in steel pipe manufacturing.

title: Deutsche Bank upgrades Prysmian to “buy” on AI demand
summary: Deutsche Bank upgraded Prysmian SpA to “buy” from “hold” and raised its price target to €167 from €100. The report links the upgrade to stronger demand tied to AI infrastructure, which is relevant to cable makers like Prysmian.
